name: Pate Sucree
recipeIngredient: 8 tablespoons cold unsalted butter, cut into bits
recipeInstructions:
1 In a large bowl with your fingertips or with two knives blend the flour, sugar, salt, and the butter until the mixture resembles meal.
2 Add the egg and 1 tablespoon of the water, tossing the mixture until the liquid is incorporated, adding 1 more tablespoon of water if the mixture is dry.
3 Form the dough into a ball.
4 Knead the dough lightly with the heel of your hand against a smooth surface for a few seconds to distribute the fat evenly and re-form it into a ball.
5 Dust the dough with flour and chill it, wrapped in waxed paper, for 1 hour.
PATE SUCREE: PROCESSOR METHOD
1 In a food processor fitted with the plastic or steel blade combine the flour, sugar, salt, and butter and process until it resembles coarse meal.
2 In a bowl combine the egg with 1 tablespoon of the water and sprinkle over the flour mixture.
3 Process the mixture until it comes together in large pieces about the size of pebbles and transfer to a work surface. If the mixture is dry, sprinkle it with 1 more tablespoon water.
4 With the heel of your hand knead the dough against a smooth surface for a few seconds to distribute the fat evenly and form it into a ball.
5 Dust the dough with flour and chill it, wrapped in waxed paper, for at least 1 hour or up to 2 days.
Yield: about 15 ounces dough or enough dough for one 10-inch shell
